clusters.title: Trump heading to China for high-stakes meeting with Xi
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump heading to China for high-stakes meeting with Xi
bullet_summary: US President Donald ⁠Trump said he expects to have a "long talk" about Iran with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping when he arrives in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit
President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons
Trump said that the two leaders will also have a "long talk" about Iran and that he would speak to Xi about US arms sales to Taiwan

items.title: Trump heading to China for high-stakes meeting with Xi
items.standfirst: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
items.content: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

items.title: Trade, Taiwan and Iran cast shadows on Trump’s China summit with Xi
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

items.title: Middle East live: Trump says stopping Iran's nuclear programme outweighs Americans' economic pain
items.standfirst: US President Donald ⁠Trump said he expects to have a "long talk" about Iran with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping when he arrives in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit. Trump said Americans’ financial struggles were not a factor in his decision-making as he seeks to negotiate an end ​to the war, adding that preventing Tehran from acquiring a nuclear weapon is his top priority. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: US President Donald ⁠Trump said he expects to have a "long talk" about Iran with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping when he arrives in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit. Trump said Americans’ financial struggles were not a factor in his decision-making as he seeks to negotiate an end ​to the war, adding that preventing Tehran from acquiring a nuclear weapon is his top priority. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Trump on "the only thing that matters" in dealing with Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons.
items.content: President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons.

items.title: What's Trump's game plan in China amid Iran war?
items.standfirst: President Trump headed to China on Tuesday for a high-stakes and highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before leaving, Mr. Trump downplayed the need for Xi to intervene in the stalled peace talks with Iran. Anna Coren reports from Beijing.
items.content: President Trump headed to China on Tuesday for a high-stakes and highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before leaving, Mr. Trump downplayed the need for Xi to intervene in the stalled peace talks with Iran. Anna Coren reports from Beijing.

items.title: Trump heads to high-stakes summit in China
items.standfirst: President Trump has left for Beijing and a high-stakes summit with China’s President Xi. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas is there.
items.content: President Trump has left for Beijing and a high-stakes summit with China’s President Xi. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas is there.

items.title: Trump 'could choose to change Taiwan policy in Beijing' unhindered by Congress, analyst says
items.standfirst: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, David Sacks, Fellow for Asia studies at the Council on Foreign Relations, explains that Trump "could choose to change US policy towards Taiwan and Congress only has so many things that it can do to try to rein that in". U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese ​President Xi ‌Jinping are holding two days of meetings on Thursday and ⁠Friday where the issue of Taiwan, which Beijing views as its own territory, is certain to come up.
items.content: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, David Sacks, Fellow for Asia studies at the Council on Foreign Relations, explains that Trump "could choose to change US policy towards Taiwan and Congress only has so many things that it can do to try to rein that in". U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese ​President Xi ‌Jinping are holding two days of meetings on Thursday and ⁠Friday where the issue of Taiwan, which Beijing views as its own territory, is certain to come up.

items.title: Trump says he’s not thinking about Americans’ finances ‘even a little bit’ in Iran talks
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump told reporters on Tuesday that he is not weighing the economic burden of the Iran war on everyday Americans when negotiating a deal with the country’s leadership
items.content: President Donald Trump told reporters on Tuesday that he is not weighing the economic burden of the Iran war on everyday Americans when negotiating a deal with the country’s leadership

items.title: Trump heads to China in bid to boost trade, reduce Iran and Taiwan tensions
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump is expected to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday where he will meet with Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping to discuss increasing trade between the two countries. Trump said that the two leaders will also have a "long talk" about Iran and that he would speak to Xi about US arms sales to Taiwan.
items.content: US President Donald Trump is expected to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday where he will meet with Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping to discuss increasing trade between the two countries. Trump said that the two leaders will also have a "long talk" about Iran and that he would speak to Xi about US arms sales to Taiwan.

clusters.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
clusters.neutral_headline: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
bullet_summary: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday
Reported by BBC World, NPR News, CBS News

items.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
items.standfirst: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.
items.content: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.

items.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 of brain tumor
items.standfirst: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.
items.content: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)

clusters.title: Japanese crisp bags turn black and white due to Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: Snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
bullet_summary: Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May
Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Japanese crisp bags turn black and white due to Iran war
items.standfirst: Global oil prices rose further with Brent crude climbing near $110 a barrel, as a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz continues with the US and Iran both rejecting the other's ceasefire proposal. Meanwhile, Japanese snack giant Calbee has decided to temporarily switch to black and white packaging for some of its products including crisps and prawn crackers due to supply instability in printing ink, made using petrochemical materials.
items.content: Global oil prices rose further with Brent crude climbing near $110 a barrel, as a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz continues with the US and Iran both rejecting the other's ceasefire proposal. Meanwhile, Japanese snack giant Calbee has decided to temporarily switch to black and white packaging for some of its products including crisps and prawn crackers due to supply instability in printing ink, made using petrochemical materials.

items.title: Snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
items.standfirst: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.
items.content: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.

items.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
items.standfirst: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue reading...
items.content: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. clusters.title: NAACP president calls Supreme Court redistricting decision "one of the worst" in history
clusters.neutral_headline: NAACP president calls Supreme Court redistricting decision "one of...
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NPR News

items.title: NAACP president calls Supreme Court redistricting decision "one of the worst" in history
items.standfirst: Republican Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey is pushing back primaries for four Congressional districts following Monday's Supreme Court decision allowing the state to use a 2023 map with only one majority-Black district. Other states are also looking to redraw their maps after the decision. NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.
items.content: Republican Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey is pushing back primaries for four Congressional districts following Monday's Supreme Court decision allowing the state to use a 2023 map with only one majority-Black district. Other states are also looking to redraw their maps after the decision. NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

items.title: Alabama splits U.S. House primaries after court ruling; S.C. redistricting stalls
items.standfirst: Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the Supreme Court cleared the way — and as redistricting ripples through the South.
items.content: Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the Supreme Court cleared the way — and as redistricting ripples through the South.(Image credit: Kim Chandler)

clusters.title: Emmanuel Macron wants dialogue with Rwanda and DRC
clusters.neutral_headline: Macron unveils $27 billion Africa investment, urges reset with Europe

items.title: Emmanuel Macron wants dialogue with Rwanda and DRC
items.standfirst: “If now everyone is rushing to isolate Rwanda simply because the Americans have done so, there is little chance of persuading Rwanda to adopt a cooperative policy.” In an exclusive interview with FRANCE 24, RFI and TV5Monde, French President Emmanuel Macron called for dialogue with Rwanda and DR Congo
items.content: “If now everyone is rushing to isolate Rwanda simply because the Americans have done so, there is little chance of persuading Rwanda to adopt a cooperative policy.” In an exclusive interview with FRANCE 24, RFI and TV5Monde, French President Emmanuel Macron called for dialogue with Rwanda and DR Congo

items.title: Emmanuel Macron’s speaks about France's policies in Sahel countries
items.standfirst: "I think we should have had that challenging dialogue sooner. And perhaps in such cases, we should have rethought our military presence sooner." In his interview with FRANCE 24, RFI and TV5Monde, French President Emmanuel Macron reflected on the state of relations between France and the Sahel countries. Here's a part of his interview.
items.content: "I think we should have had that challenging dialogue sooner. And perhaps in such cases, we should have rethought our military presence sooner." items.title: Mali, immigration and DR Congo: Five key takeaways from Macron's interview with FRANCE 24
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron on Tuesday spoke to FRANCE 24 at the close of the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, Kenya. Here’s what you need to know about how Macron sees France’s changing relationship with African nations.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron on Tuesday spoke to FRANCE 24 at the close of the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, Kenya. Here’s what you need to know about how Macron sees France’s changing relationship with African nations.

items.title: Macron unveils $27 billion Africa investment, urges reset with Europe
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron, in Kenya for the Africa Forward summit, called for a ‘fundamental reset’ in Africa.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron, in Kenya for the Africa Forward summit, called for a ‘fundamental reset’ in Africa.

items.title: Replay: Macron speaks to FRANCE 24 as Africa Forward Summit wraps up
items.standfirst: As the Africa Forward Summit came to a close, French President Emmanuel Macron spoke to journalists from FRANCE 24, RFI and TV5Monde in the Kenyan capital of Nairobi about what the president described as a new model of partnership between Paris and African nations. Watch the full interview on FRANCE 24.
items.content: As the Africa Forward Summit came to a close, French President Emmanuel Macron spoke to journalists from FRANCE 24, RFI and TV5Monde in the Kenyan capital of Nairobi about what the president described as a new model of partnership between Paris and African nations. Watch the full interview on FRANCE 24.

clusters.neutral_headline: Hamas 'weaponised' sexual violence in 7 October attacks, Israeli investigation says

items.title: Hamas 'weaponised' sexual violence in 7 October attacks, Israeli investigation says
items.standfirst: The most comprehensive report of its kind details harrowing cases of sexual attacks which it says were systematic.
items.content: The most comprehensive report of its kind details harrowing cases of sexual attacks which it says were systematic.

items.title: Israeli report says Hamas used 'systematic, widespread' sexual violence in October 7 attack
items.standfirst: A report released on Tuesday by an independent Israeli investigative commission found that sexual violence was a "central component" of Hamas' October 7, 2023 attack on Israel and of the captivity of the hostages that the militant group took back to Gaza.
items.content: A report released on Tuesday by an independent Israeli investigative commission found that sexual violence was a "central component" of Hamas' October 7, 2023 attack on Israel and of the captivity of the hostages that the militant group took back to Gaza.
